2603.21460.title: When Documents Disagree: Measuring Institutional Variation in Transplant Guidance with Retrieval-Augmented Language Models
2603.21460.paper_summary: Patient education materials for solid-organ transplantation vary substantially across U.S. centers, yet no systematic method exists to quantify this heterogeneity at scale. We introduce a framework that grounds the same patient questions in different centers' handbooks using retrieval-augmented language models and compares the resulting answers using a five-label consistency taxonomy. Applied to 102 handbooks from 23 centers and 1,115 benchmark questions, the framework quantifies heterogeneity across four dimensions: question, topic, organ, and center. We find that 20.8% of non-absent pairwise comparisons exhibit clinically meaningful divergence, concentrated in condition monitoring and lifestyle topics. Coverage gaps are even more prominent: 96.2% of question-handbook pairs miss relevant content, with reproductive health at 95.1% absence. Center-level divergence profiles are stable and interpretable, where heterogeneity reflects systematic institutional differences, likely due to patient diversity. These findings expose an information gap in transplant patient education materials, with document-grounded medical question answering highlighting opportunities for content improvement.
2603.21460.paper_summary_zh: 病人教育材料在美國各中心的實體器官移植中差異顯著，但目前尚無系統性方法能夠在大規模上量化這種異質性。我們引入了一個框架，將相同的病人問題根據不同中心的手冊進行基礎設置，使用增強檢索的語言模型，並通過五標籤一致性分類法比較結果答案。該框架應用於來自23個中心的102本手冊和1,115個基準問題，量化了四個維度的異質性：問題、主題、器官和中心。我們發現20.8%的非缺失成對比較顯示出臨床上有意義的差異，主要集中在病情監測和生活方式主題上。覆蓋差距更為明顯：96.2%的問題-手冊對缺少相關內容，生殖健康的缺失率高達95.1%。中心層面的差異特徵是穩定且可解釋的，異質性反映了系統性的機構差異，可能是由於病人多樣性造成的。這些發現揭示了移植病人教育材料中的信息缺口，基於文件的醫學問題回答突顯了內容改進的機會。
2603.21460.author: Yubo Li et.al.
2603.21460.authors: Yubo Li, Ramayya Krishnan, Rema Padman
